discussion_posts.name: butterman1986
discussion_posts.content: I felt that the problem statement was difficult to understand as it and the associated example don't clearly indicate what surrounded means. An 'O' is surrounded if there is **NO** path from it to the boundary of the matrix (ie. row index 0, column index 0, row index matrix.length-1, column index matrix[0].length-1) when moving in a North, South, East, or West direction :). Hope this helps anyone else who was unclear.

discussion_posts.name: piyushsharmaleetcode
discussion_posts.content:
1) All the "O" that are not directly/indirectly connected to the "O" on the boundary line, will be captured and converted into "X"
2) Just traverse the matrix once, and as soon as your enounter a boundary line that has a "O" in it, run DFS on this "O" and "Save" all the nehgbours of this "O" from being captured by calling them "S"
3) Now traverse the matrix again and capture all the "O" that you can inside the matrix except the boundary line
4) Rename the "Save" O back to "O"
5) Done!
